title: A2A (Agent-to-Agent Protocol)
existing_short_definition: Google's open protocol (announced 2025) for agents from different vendors to discover, authenticate, and exchange tasks.

title: Admin API (OpenAI / Anthropic)
existing_short_definition: Management APIs for LLM providers that handle projects, teams, API keys, usage limits, and billing — separate from inference APIs.

title: Agent Card
existing_short_definition: A JSON document that describes an agent's capabilities, auth, and endpoints in the A2A protocol — discoverable at a well-known URL.

title: Anthropic API Prompt Caching
existing_short_definition: Claude's explicit prompt-caching mechanism — mark specific content blocks with cache_control and pay a reduced rate on subsequent calls with matching prefixes.

title: API
existing_short_definition: A way for software to talk to other software — in AI, it's how you send prompts to a model and get responses back programmatically.

title: Assistants API (OpenAI)
existing_short_definition: OpenAI's stateful API (predecessor to Responses API) for building assistants with persistent threads, file search, and code interpreter.

title: Batch API
existing_short_definition: An async LLM API that accepts a file of requests, processes them offline within 24 hours, and returns results — typically at ~50% cost of sync.

title: Chat Completions API
existing_short_definition: The OpenAI-style API format for chat-based LLM calls — a list of role-tagged messages in, a new assistant message out.

title: Embeddings API
existing_short_definition: The API endpoint provided by LLM vendors for converting text (and increasingly images) to vector embeddings for search, classification, and clustering.

title: gRPC (LLM Serving)
existing_short_definition: A high-performance RPC framework using HTTP/2 and protobuf — common for internal LLM serving where JSON overhead matters.

title: JSON Schema Enforcement
existing_short_definition: Provider-side feature where the LLM's output is guaranteed to validate against a supplied JSON schema — no post-parsing retries needed.

title: LiteLLM
existing_short_definition: An open-source Python SDK and gateway that wraps 100+ LLM providers behind an OpenAI-compatible interface.

title: LLM Gateway
existing_short_definition: A middleware layer between applications and LLM providers that handles routing, caching, retries, cost tracking, and model fallback.

title: MCP (Model Context Protocol)
existing_short_definition: An open standard that lets AI assistants connect to external tools and data sources in a standardized way.

title: MCP Client
existing_short_definition: An application that connects to MCP servers and exposes their resources, prompts, and tools to an LLM for use during a session.

title: MCP Prompts
existing_short_definition: Reusable prompt templates MCP servers expose, often surfaced in client UIs as slash-commands or workflow starters.

title: MCP Resources
existing_short_definition: Read-only context MCP servers expose to LLMs — files, database rows, API responses — identified by URI.

title: MCP Sampling
existing_short_definition: An MCP feature that lets a server request LLM completions through the client, so server authors can build LLM-backed workflows without bundling a model.

title: MCP Server
existing_short_definition: A program that implements the Model Context Protocol server interface, exposing resources, prompts, and tools for MCP-compatible LLM clients to consume.

title: MCP Tools
existing_short_definition: Executable actions an MCP server exposes to LLMs — the subset of MCP capabilities meant for side-effecting or interactive operations.

title: Messages API (Anthropic)
existing_short_definition: Anthropic's native API format for Claude — a messages array plus a separate system field, distinct from OpenAI's chat completions format.

title: OpenAI Batch API (Reprise)
existing_short_definition: OpenAI's 50%-discounted asynchronous API for dataset-scale jobs — submit a JSONL file, retrieve results within 24 hours.

title: OpenAPI Tool Binding
existing_short_definition: Automatically generating LLM-callable tool definitions from an OpenAPI (Swagger) specification — an instant way to expose a REST API to agents.

title: Function Calling (Parallel)
existing_short_definition: LLM feature where the model emits multiple tool calls in a single response, to be executed concurrently by the client.

title: Prompt Caching (API)
existing_short_definition: Reusing the computed KV cache of a shared prompt prefix across multiple API calls, cutting cost and latency for repeated context.

title: Rate Limit (LLM APIs)
existing_short_definition: Provider-enforced caps on request frequency and total tokens per minute or day, controlling cost and protecting infrastructure.

title: Realtime API (OpenAI)
existing_short_definition: OpenAI's WebSocket-based API for speech-in, speech-out conversations with sub-second latency and native turn-taking / interruption.

title: Responses API (OpenAI)
existing_short_definition: OpenAI's newer stateful API for multi-turn interactions, tool use, and reasoning — a superset of the Chat Completions format.

title: Responses API Reasoning (OpenAI)
existing_short_definition: OpenAI's Responses API surface for reasoning models — provides reasoning_effort control, persistent reasoning summaries, and tool-use integration.

title: Server-Sent Events (SSE)
existing_short_definition: An HTTP standard for streaming updates from server to client over a long-lived connection — the transport LLM APIs use for token streaming.

title: Streaming (Token Streaming)
existing_short_definition: Returning model output token-by-token as it's generated, instead of waiting for the full response — dramatically improves perceived responsiveness in chat UIs.

title: Strict Mode (OpenAI)
existing_short_definition: OpenAI's enforcement flag for function calling and JSON schema that guarantees output exactly matches the schema, at the cost of reduced vocabulary flexibility.

title: Structured Output (JSON Mode)
existing_short_definition: Forcing an LLM to output valid JSON (or another strict format) that matches a schema — the reliable way to integrate LLMs into deterministic pipelines.
existing_code_example:
# Instructor with Pydantic
from pydantic import BaseModel
import instructor
from openai import OpenAI

client = instructor.from_openai(OpenAI())

class Review(BaseModel):
    sentiment: Literal['positive', 'negative', 'neutral']
    summary: str

review = client.chat.completions.create(
    model='gpt-4o-mini',
    response_model=Review,
    messages=[{'role':'user','content':'Analyze: ...'}],
    max_retries=2,
)

title: Tool Choice
existing_short_definition: An API parameter controlling how the model selects tools — auto, required (must use a tool), none (forbidden), or a specific named tool.

title: Tool Use (Function Calling)
existing_short_definition: The LLM capability to decide when to call external functions, choose arguments, and incorporate the returned results back into its reasoning — the foundation of agentic behavior.
existing_code_example:
# Anthropic tool use
response = client.messages.create(
    model='claude-3-5-sonnet-latest',
    max_tokens=1024,
    tools=[{
        'name': 'get_weather',
        'description': 'Get current weather in a city',
        'input_schema': {'type':'object','properties':{'city':{'type':'string'}},'required':['city']},
    }],
    messages=[{'role':'user','content':'Weather in Paris?'}],
)
# response.stop_reason == 'tool_use' → extract, execute, return

title: Webhook (LLM Integrations)
existing_short_definition: An HTTP callback mechanism where a service POSTs event data to a URL you register, used extensively for async LLM completions, tool events, and integrations.

title: WebSockets (LLM)
existing_short_definition: A bidirectional protocol over a persistent TCP connection — used for real-time LLM interactions, voice, and multimodal streaming.
